meta.ministry: Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances
meta.project_subject: Work Order to work in association with DARPG in successfully conducting the IIAS-DARPG Conference in February, 2025 by being the nodal point for planning and working on actionable agendas as prioritized by the Department
meta.deliverables: The PMU will act as the nodal point for planning and executing actionable agendas prioritized by DARPG for the IIAS-DARPG Conference in February 2025. It will draft agendas for review meetings, generate regular reports and publications as per pre-defined timelines, handle dissemination, and undertake any other tasks assigned by senior officers on a day-to-day basis to ensure coordination among DARPG, IIAS, and participants.

content.full_text: The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) issues this work order to the Quality Council of India (QCI) dated 13 December 2024 for setting up a Project Monitoring Unit (PMU) to coordinate the IIAS-DARPG Conference scheduled for February 2025. QCI will serve as the nodal agency for planning and executing actionable agendas prioritized by DARPG. The PMU, comprising 3 QCI professionals working 60 man-days each at Rs 7,500 per day plus 12% administrative cost, will operate for 3 months from work-order issuance. Total project cost is Rs 15,12,000 (GST extra). Payments are milestone-based: 25% each on award, completion of 1 month, 2 months, and 3 months. DARPG may terminate services at any time if unsatisfactory.
